content_en:
From *Awn Ibn *Abdullaah Ibn * Utbah: "Sit with the Oft-repentent (Al-Tawwaaboon), for they are the softest-hearted of people."

From Aslam, ~Umar's freedman: "Be the vessels of the

Book and the-well-springs of knowledge, and ask Allah for your sustenance day by day."

From Abu ~Uthmaan An-Nahdi: "Winter is the treasure of devotees."

From Yahya Ibn Abu Katheer: "If it were announced from heaven: 'O people! You will all be admitted to Paradise except one,' I would fear to be he; and if it were announced: 'O people! You will all enter Hellfire except one,' I would hope to be he."

As we bid this righteous companion goodbye, we can only pray: May Allah handsomely reward you for all that you did for Islam and Muslims. The story of your life abounds in lessons for us and for the entire world to contemplate. You have certainly managed to leave an indelible mark in the history of mankind. No wonder that all people, be they Muslims or nonMuslims, can only marvel at such a unique person, the light of whom has never trodden the earth thereafter.

may Allah be pleased with him

"Should I not be bashful of a man in whose presence even the Angels feel bashful."

*Uthmaan Earns the Title Dhun-Noorayn (Owner of the Two Lights)

The Angels Feel Bashful in the Presence Of *Uthmaan Hadiths on the Virtues of *Uthmaan

*Uthmaan as a Great Scholar

The First Case before ~Uthmaan

Conquests in the Life-Time of *Uthmaan

Rebellion of Azerbaijan and Armenia

Byzantine Invasion of Alexandria

Conquest of Khurasan and Tabrastan

Conquest of North Africa

The Conquest of Cyprus

The Battle of Dhaat As-Sawaari (31 652 AC) Uthman's Pioneering acts

Official Manuscript of the Glorious Quran

Extension of the Prophet's Mosque

Who Is “Abdullaah Tbn Saba’? And What Is His Relation to the Sedition?

The Caliph's Meeting with the Governors of Provinces

¢ The Siege of Caliph’s House and His Martyrdom

Born six years after the year of the elephant (*Aam AlFeel), ~Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan, may Allah be pleased with him, belonged to a well-reputed and honorable family of Makkah in the period of Jahiliyyah, Banu Umayyah, a branch of the tribe of Quraysh. His ancestral pedigree joins with that of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him in the fifth generation. His father’s name is ~ Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan Ibn Abi Al--Aas Ibn Ummayyah Ibn ~Abd Shams Ibn * Abd Manaaf. His mother's name is Arwa Bint Kurayz. *Uthmaan was also known by other names, namely Abu *Abdullaah and Abu Laylah.

*Uthmaan was one of the few persons in Makkah who learned how to read and write at an early age, and as a young man became a successful merchant and businessman. Of all his contemporaries, he was a venerable man of overrated virtue and outstanding attributes. Though he was extremely wealthy and of high rank, he was always noted for his modesty and humbleness. Even before landing on the safe shore of Islam, he was a soft natured and kind hearted man; he used to help the needy and the poor-stricken, and did not hesitate to spend any amount of money on seeing a man in trouble in order to remove his misery. For his lofty morals and descent way of expression, the Makkans had great respect for him. It is also said that he never wronged anyone, nor did anything obscene, or prostrated himself before idols in Jahiliyyah.

It is said that *Uthmaan, may Allah be pleased with him, was a little above average height, extremely handsome, a fairskinned man_ with a touch of redness in his face, brunet, large-

jointed, broad-shouldered, with a thick beard which he dyed yellow, and long hair which reached his shoulders, and goldbraced teeth.

~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Hazm Al-Muzanyy is reported to have said: "I saw ~Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan, and I never saw a man or a woman handsomer of face than him." (Reported by [bn * Asaakir.)

*Uthmaan, may Allah be pleased with him, was among the early converts to Islam, He and Abu Bakr were close friends, and it was Abu Bakr who convinced him to embrace Islam, when the latter was thirty-four years of age.

It is narrated that when * Uthmaan was informed about Prophet Muhammad's giving his daughter Rugayyah in marriage to his paternal cousin ~Utbah [bn Abi Lahab, he regretted he had not asked for her hand before him, because everyone wanted to marry her because of her good manners and noble descent. ~Uthmaan went home one day in a_ state of sorrow to find there his maternal aunt Su’ da Bint Kurayz, who was a very wise and old woman. She gave him the glad tidings that a Prophet would be sent to abolish idol worship and call to monotheism, and she started calling him to the new faith, which she said would fulfil his desires.

~Uthmaan narrates: | went away thinking of my aunt's words. I met Abu Bakr and told him of what she said, whereupon Abu Bakr said to me: "By Allah, your aunt has spoken the truth, and has given you glad tidings. O ~ Uthmaan! You are a wise man of sound opinion. You know exactly where the truth lies, and falsehood cannot find way to your heart." Then he said to me: "What are those idols which our people

take as lords?! Are they not idols which neither see nor hear, and do not respond to our prayer?! "Definitely yes,” | replied. Abu Bakr continued, “What your aunt foretold has come true: Allah has sent His Messenger to all mankind with a religion of truth and guidance." | asked, "Who is he?” Abu Bakr answered, "He is Muhammad Ibn *Abdellaah Ibn ~ Abdel Muttalib." I wondered, "The Truthful and Trustworthy?!" "Yes, he is," said Abu Bakr. I said, "Would you accompany me to him?" Abu Bakr accepted, and we went together to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. When the Prophet saw me he said, "Respond to Allah's call, O ~Uthmaan, for | am Allah Messenger to you in particular and to all people in general."

~Uthmaan added: "By Allah, no sooner had I looked at him and listened to his words than he become the apple of my eye and found his way quickly to my heart. | therefore did not hesitate to acknowledge his prophethood and believe in him. I pronounced Shahadah: there is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah.

When he accepted Islam, the Quraysh who once loved ~Uthmaan became his enemies. Even his relatives began to rebuke him and chastised him severely.

One of the daughters of the Prophet, Ruqayyah was married to one of the sons of Abu Lahab (an arch-enemy of Islam). When the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, started to preach Islam, Abu Lahab asked his son * Utbah to divorce her. No sooner had * Uthmaan heard of the divorce of Rugayyah than he rushed to ask for her hand, and the Prophet in turn gave her in marriage to him. Thus he fulfilled his longestablished desire.

*“Uthmaan Earns the Title Dhun-Noorayn (Owner of the Two Lights):

“Uthmaan, may Allah be pleased with him, earned the title Dhun-Noorayn, or owner of the two lights, because he enjoyed the high privilege of entering into matrimonial alliance with two daughters of Prophet Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him. These two daughters were the lights which illuminated his house, and thus he was called Dhun-Noorayn. First he married Ruqayyah following her getting divorced from *Utbah, and she died during the battle of Badr. Ruqayyah was seriously ill just before the battle of Badr, and ~Uthmaan sought the Prophet's permission to stay behind with a view to looking after her. The Prophet excused him from participating in the battle, and further gave him the glad tidings that he would be rewarded as though he had taken part in the battle.

Then illness of Rugqayyah proved fatal and finally she passed away. At the time of burial, the news came that Muslims gained victory over Quraysh. Yet, the death of Ruqayyah had left ~Uthmaan deeply grieved, so the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, offered him the hand of his second daughter, Umm Kulthoom, *Uthmaan accepted the Prophet's proposal and married his second daughter. The latter lived with him until she died on the ninth year after Hijrah.

Scholars state that no one except * Uthmaan is known to have married two daughters of a Prophet, and because of this he was called Dhun-Noorayn.

The Angels Feel Bashful in the Presence Of *Uthmaan:

*Uthmaan, may Allah be pleased with him, was a very pious Companion and a man of high character, He was the most

bashful of all the Companions of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him.

The Mother of the Believers, ~Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, narrates: The Prophet was lying down one day in my apartment with his thighs (or shanks) exposed. Then Abu Bakr came and sought permission to enter, and the Prophet bade him come in, in that very state of his. Abu Bakr came in and spoke to him and, having his need fulfilled, went back. Then, “Umar came, sought permission to enter, and the Prophet permitted him to enter while he was in that state. *Umar entered and spoke to the Prophet and, having his need fulfilled, went back. Then, ~Uthmaan came and sought permission to enter, whereupon the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, sat up, straightened his clothing, and then let him enter. ~Uthmaan came in and spoke to the Prophet. After he had gone, 1 asked the Prophet: "Abu Bakr came in and you neither sat up nor show any anxiety (with regard to dressing), and ~ Umar came in and you neither sat up nor show any anxiety. But when ~Uthmaan came in, you straightened out your clothing?! The Prophet said: "Should I not be bashful of a man in whose presence even the angels feel bashful?” (Reported by Moslem.)

Hadiths on the Virtues of ~Uthmaan:

In addition to the Hadiths mentioned earlier, the following Hadiths may be quoted in order to confirm the virtues and outstanding merits of * Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan, may Allah be pleased with him.

Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, said: "*Uthmaan" purchased Paradise from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, two times: when he dug the well of Roomah and when he equipped the army of Al-* Usrah." (Reported by Al-Haakim.)

Abu Hurayrah also narrates that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "“Uthmaan is the most of my Companions in resembling my character." (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.) :
content_id:
Dari *Awn Ibn *Abdullaah Ibn *Utbah: "Duduklah bersama orang-orang yang banyak bertaubat (Al-Tawwaaboon), karena mereka adalah orang-orang yang paling lembut hatinya."

Dari Aslam, mantan budak *Umar: "Jadilah wadah-wadah bagi

Kitab dan mata air ilmu pengetahuan, dan mohonlah kepada Allah rezeki kalian hari demi hari."

Dari Abu *Uthmaan An-Nahdi: "Musim dingin adalah harta karun para ahli ibadah."

Dari Yahya Ibn Abu Katheer: "Seandainya diumumkan dari langit: 'Wahai manusia! Kalian semua akan masuk Surga kecuali satu orang,' aku akan takut menjadi orang itu; dan seandainya diumumkan: 'Wahai manusia! Kalian semua akan masuk Neraka kecuali satu orang,' aku akan berharap menjadi orang itu."

Ketika kita mengucapkan selamat tinggal kepada sahabat yang saleh ini, kita hanya dapat berdoa: Semoga Allah membalas dengan sebaik-baik balasan atas semua yang telah engkau lakukan untuk Islam dan kaum muslimin. Kisah hidupmu penuh dengan pelajaran bagi kita dan bagi seluruh dunia untuk direnungkan. Engkau sungguh telah berhasil meninggalkan jejak yang tak terhapuskan dalam sejarah umat manusia. Tidak mengherankan bahwa semua orang, baik Muslim maupun non-Muslim, hanya dapat terkagum-kagum pada sosok yang begitu istimewa ini, yang cahayanya tidak pernah ada duanya di muka bumi setelahnya.

semoga Allah meridhainya

"Tidakkah aku seharusnya malu kepada seorang lelaki yang bahkan para Malaikat pun merasa malu di hadapannya."

*Uthmaan Mendapat Gelar Dhun-Noorayn (Pemilik Dua Cahaya)

Para Malaikat Merasa Malu di Hadapan *Uthmaan — Hadith-hadith tentang Keutamaan *Uthmaan

*Uthmaan sebagai Seorang Ulama Besar

Kasus Pertama di Hadapan *Uthmaan

Penaklukan-penaklukan pada Masa *Uthmaan

Pemberontakan Azerbaijan dan Armenia

Invasi Byzantium ke Alexandria

Penaklukan Khurasan dan Tabrastan

Penaklukan Afrika Utara

Penaklukan Siprus

Pertempuran Dhaat As-Sawaari (31 — 652 M) — Tindakan-tindakan Pelopor Utsman

Naskah Resmi Al-Quran Al-Karim

Perluasan Masjid Nabawi

Siapakah *Abdullaah Ibn Saba'? Dan Apa Hubungannya dengan Fitnah?

Pertemuan Khalifah dengan Para Gubernur Provinsi

¢ Pengepungan Rumah Khalifah dan Syahidnya Beliau

Lahir enam tahun setelah tahun gajah (*Aam Al-Feel), *Uthmaan Ibn *Affaan, semoga Allah meridhainya, berasal dari keluarga terpandang dan terhormat di Makkah pada masa Jahiliyyah, Banu Umayyah, cabang dari suku Quraysh. Silsilah keturunannya bertemu dengan silsilah Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau, pada generasi kelima. Nama ayahnya adalah *Uthmaan Ibn *Affaan Ibn Abi Al-*Aas Ibn Ummayyah Ibn *Abd Shams Ibn *Abd Manaaf. Nama ibunya adalah Arwa Bint Kurayz. *Uthmaan juga dikenal dengan nama-nama lain, yaitu Abu *Abdullaah dan Abu Laylah.

*Uthmaan adalah salah satu dari sedikit orang di Makkah yang belajar membaca dan menulis sejak usia dini, dan ketika masih muda menjadi seorang pedagang dan pengusaha yang sukses. Di antara semua orang sezamannya, beliau adalah orang yang sangat terhormat dengan keutamaan yang tinggi dan sifat-sifat yang menonjol. Meskipun beliau sangat kaya dan berkedudukan tinggi, beliau selalu dikenal karena kerendahan hati dan kesederhanaannya. Bahkan sebelum berlabuh di pantai Islam yang aman, beliau adalah seorang yang berwatak lembut dan baik hati; beliau biasa membantu orang-orang yang membutuhkan dan yang tertimpa kemiskinan, dan tidak ragu mengeluarkan uang seberapapun ketika melihat seseorang dalam kesulitan untuk meringankan penderitaannya. Karena akhlaknya yang mulia dan cara perkataannya yang santun, orang-orang Makkah sangat menghormatinya. Dikatakan pula bahwa beliau tidak pernah menzalimi siapapun, tidak pernah melakukan hal yang keji, dan tidak pernah bersujud kepada berhala-berhala pada masa Jahiliyyah.

Dikatakan bahwa *Uthmaan, semoga Allah meridhainya, memiliki postur sedikit di atas rata-rata, sangat tampan, berkulit putih dengan sedikit kemerahan di wajahnya, berambut gelap, berbadan besar,

berbahu lebar, dengan janggut tebal yang beliau warnai kuning, dan rambut panjang yang menjuntai hingga bahunya, serta gigi yang dihiasi emas.

*Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Hazm Al-Muzanyy dilaporkan berkata: "Aku pernah melihat *Uthmaan Ibn *Affaan, dan aku tidak pernah melihat seorang lelaki atau perempuan yang lebih tampan wajahnya darinya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn *Asaakir.)

*Uthmaan, semoga Allah meridhainya, termasuk di antara orang-orang yang pertama kali masuk Islam. Beliau dan Abu Bakr adalah sahabat karib, dan Abu Bakr lah yang meyakinkannya untuk memeluk Islam, ketika yang terakhir ini berusia tiga puluh empat tahun.

Diriwayatkan bahwa ketika *Uthmaan mendengar bahwa Nabi Muhammad menikahkan putrinya Ruqayyah kepada saudara sepupunya *Utbah Ibn Abi Lahab, beliau menyesal tidak meminta tangan Ruqayyah sebelumnya, karena setiap orang ingin menikahinya karena akhlaknya yang baik dan keturunannya yang mulia. *Uthmaan pulang ke rumah suatu hari dalam keadaan bersedih dan mendapati bibi dari pihak ibunya, Su'da Bint Kurayz, yang merupakan seorang wanita tua yang sangat bijaksana, sedang berada di sana. Sang bibi menyampaikan kabar gembira bahwa seorang Nabi akan diutus untuk menghapuskan penyembahan berhala dan menyeru kepada tauhid, dan beliau mulai mengajaknya kepada keimanan baru itu, yang katanya akan memenuhi keinginannya.

*Uthmaan bercerita: Aku pergi sambil memikirkan kata-kata bibiku. Aku bertemu Abu Bakr dan menceritakan kepadanya apa yang dikatakan bibiku, lalu Abu Bakr berkata kepadaku: "Demi Allah, bibim telah berkata benar, dan telah memberimu kabar gembira. Wahai *Uthmaan! Engkau adalah orang yang bijaksana dan berpendapat sehat. Engkau tahu persis di mana kebenaran itu berada, dan kebatilan tidak dapat menemukan jalan ke hatimu." Kemudian beliau berkata kepadaku: "Apa berhala-berhala itu yang dijadikan tuhan oleh kaum kita?! Bukankah mereka hanyalah berhala-berhala yang tidak melihat dan tidak mendengar, dan tidak menjawab doa kita?!" "Tentu saja," jawabku. Abu Bakr melanjutkan, "Apa yang diramalkan bibim telah terbukti: Allah telah mengutus Rasul-Nya kepada seluruh umat manusia dengan agama yang benar dan hidayah." Aku bertanya, "Siapakah dia?" Abu Bakr menjawab, "Dialah Muhammad Ibn *Abdullaah Ibn *Abdel Muttalib." Aku terheran-heran, "Al-Shadiq wal-Amin (Yang Jujur dan Terpercaya)?!" "Ya, dialah," kata Abu Bakr. Aku berkata, "Maukah engkau menemaniku menemuinya?" Abu Bakr menyetujui, dan kami pergi bersama menemui Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau. Ketika Nabi melihatku, beliau bersabda, "Sambutlah seruan Allah, wahai *Uthmaan, karena aku adalah Rasul Allah untukmu khususnya dan untuk semua orang pada umumnya."

*Uthmaan menambahkan: "Demi Allah, begitu aku memandangnya dan mendengar kata-katanya, beliau langsung menjadi buah hatiku dan dengan cepat menemukan jalannya ke hatiku. Karena itu aku tidak ragu untuk mengakui kenabiannya dan beriman kepadanya. Aku mengucapkan Syahadat: tidak ada tuhan selain Allah, dan Muhammad adalah Rasulullah.

Ketika beliau menerima Islam, orang-orang Quraysh yang dahulu mencintai *Uthmaan menjadi musuh-musuhnya. Bahkan kerabatnya pun mulai mencela dan menyiksanya dengan keras.

Salah satu putri Nabi, Ruqayyah, dinikahkan dengan salah satu putra Abu Lahab (musuh besar Islam). Ketika Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau, mulai menyebarkan Islam, Abu Lahab meminta putranya *Utbah untuk menceraikannya. Begitu *Uthmaan mendengar perceraian Ruqayyah, beliau segera pergi untuk melamarnya, dan Nabi pun menikahkannya kepadanya. Demikianlah beliau memenuhi keinginannya yang telah lama terpendam.

*Uthmaan Mendapat Gelar Dhun-Noorayn (Pemilik Dua Cahaya):

*Uthmaan, semoga Allah meridhainya, mendapat gelar Dhun-Noorayn, atau pemilik dua cahaya, karena beliau menikmati keistimewaan tinggi berupa ikatan pernikahan dengan dua putri Nabi Muhammad, shalawat dan salam atas beliau. Kedua putri tersebut adalah dua cahaya yang menerangi rumahnya, sehingga beliau disebut Dhun-Noorayn. Pertama beliau menikahi Ruqayyah setelah Ruqayyah diceraikan oleh *Utbah, dan Ruqayyah wafat pada saat Perang Badar. Ruqayyah sedang sakit parah sesaat sebelum Perang Badar, dan *Uthmaan memohon izin kepada Nabi untuk tinggal di belakang guna merawatnya. Nabi mengizinkannya untuk tidak ikut dalam peperangan tersebut, dan lebih lanjut memberinya kabar gembira bahwa beliau akan mendapat pahala seolah-olah telah ikut serta dalam peperangan itu.

Kemudian sakit Ruqayyah ternyata membawa kematian dan akhirnya beliau wafat. Pada saat pemakaman, datang berita bahwa kaum muslimin meraih kemenangan atas Quraysh. Namun, kematian Ruqayyah membuat *Uthmaan berduka cita mendalam, sehingga Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau, menawarkan kepadanya tangan putri keduanya, Umm Kulthoom. *Uthmaan menerima tawaran Nabi dan menikahi putri keduanya. Yang terakhir ini tinggal bersamanya hingga beliau wafat pada tahun kesembilan setelah Hijrah.

Para ulama menyatakan bahwa tidak ada seorang pun selain *Uthmaan yang diketahui pernah menikahi dua putri seorang Nabi, dan karena itu beliau disebut Dhun-Noorayn.

Para Malaikat Merasa Malu di Hadapan *Uthmaan:

*Uthmaan, semoga Allah meridhainya, adalah seorang Sahabat yang sangat bertakwa dan seorang yang berkarakter mulia. Beliau adalah yang paling pemalu di antara semua Sahabat Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau.

Ummul Mukminin, *Aa'ishah, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: Pada suatu hari Nabi sedang berbaring di kamarku dengan paha (atau betis) beliau yang terbuka. Kemudian Abu Bakr datang dan meminta izin untuk masuk, dan Nabi mempersilakannya masuk dalam keadaan seperti itu. Abu Bakr masuk dan berbicara kepada beliau, dan setelah keperluannya terpenuhi, beliau kembali. Kemudian *Umar datang, meminta izin untuk masuk, dan Nabi mengizinkannya masuk sementara beliau dalam keadaan seperti itu. *Umar masuk dan berbicara kepada Nabi, dan setelah keperluannya terpenuhi, beliau kembali. Kemudian *Uthmaan datang dan meminta izin untuk masuk, lalu Nabi, shalawat dan salam atas beliau, duduk tegak, merapikan pakaiannya, dan kemudian mempersilakannya masuk. *Uthmaan masuk dan berbicara kepada Nabi. Setelah beliau pergi, aku bertanya kepada Nabi: "Abu Bakr masuk dan engkau tidak duduk tegak dan tidak menampakkan kehati-hatian (dalam hal berpakaian), begitu pula *Umar masuk dan engkau tidak duduk tegak dan tidak menampakkan kehati-hatian. Tetapi ketika *Uthmaan masuk, engkau merapikan pakaianmu?!" Nabi bersabda: "Tidakkah aku seharusnya malu kepada seorang lelaki yang bahkan para malaikat pun merasa malu di hadapannya?" (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Hadith-Hadith tentang Keutamaan 'Utsmaan:

Selain hadith-hadith yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya, hadith-hadith berikut dapat dikutip untuk mengukuhkan keutamaan dan keistimewaan 'Utsmaan Ibn 'Affaan, radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Abu Hurayrah, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata: "'Utsmaan membeli Surga dari Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dua kali: ketika ia menggali sumur Roomah dan ketika ia mempersiapkan pasukan Al-'Usrah." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haakim.)

Abu Hurayrah juga meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "'Utsmaan adalah orang yang paling menyerupai akhlakku di antara para Sahabatku." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asaakir.)
content_ar: From *Awn Ibn *Abdullaah Ibn * Utbah: "Sit with the Oft-repentent (Al-Tawwaaboon), for they are the softest-hearted of people." From Aslam, ~عمر's freedman: "Be the vessels of the الكتاب and the-well-springs of العلم, and ask الله for your sustenance day by day." From Abu ~Uthmaan An-Nahdi: "Winter is the treasure of devotees." From Yahya Ibn Abu Katheer: "إذا it were announced from heaven: 'O people! You will all be admitted to الجنة except one,' I would الخوف to be he; and إذا it were announced: 'O people! You will all enter النار except one,' I would الأمل to be he." As we bid this الصالح companion goodbye, we can only pray: May الله handsomely الأجر you for all that you did for الإسلام and المسلمون. The story of your الحياة abounds in lessons for us and for the entire الدنيا to contemplate. You have certainly managed to leave an indelible mark in the history of mankind. No wonder that all people, be they المسلمون or nonMuslims, can only marvel at such a unique person, the light of whom has never trodden the earth thereafter. may الله be pleased with him "Should I not be bashful of a man in whose presence even the الملائكة feel bashful." *Uthmaan Earns the Title Dhun-Noorayn (Owner of the Two Lights) The الملائكة Feel Bashful in the Presence Of *Uthmaan Hadiths on the الفضائل of *Uthmaan *Uthmaan as a Great Scholar The أولاً Case قبل ~Uthmaan Conquests in the الحياة-Time of *Uthmaan Rebellion of Azerbaijan and Armenia Byzantine Invasion of Alexandria Conquest of Khurasan and Tabrastan Conquest of North Africa The Conquest of Cyprus The Battle of Dhaat As-Sawaari (31 652 AC) عثمان's Pioneering acts Official Manuscript of the Glorious القرآن الكريم Extension of النبي ﷺ's المسجد Who Is “Abdullaah Tbn Saba’? And What Is His Relation to the Sedition? The Caliph's Meeting with the Governors of Provinces ¢ The Siege of Caliph’s House and His Martyrdom Born six years بعد the year of the elephant (*Aam AlFeel), ~Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan, may الله be pleased with him, belonged to a well-reputed and honorable الأسرة of مكة المكرمة in the period of Jahiliyyah, Banu Umayyah, a branch of the tribe of Quraysh. His ancestral pedigree joins with that of النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him in the خامساً generation. His الأب’s name is ~ Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan Ibn Abi Al--Aas Ibn Ummayyah Ibn ~Abd Shams Ibn * Abd Manaaf. His الأم's name is Arwa Bint Kurayz. *Uthmaan was أيضاً known by other names, أي Abu *Abdullaah and Abu Laylah. *Uthmaan was one of the few persons in مكة المكرمة who learned how to read and write at an early age, and as a young man became a successful merchant and businessman.
